
### checking for array bounds  {.smaller}


* can <i>try</i> to apply same technique to array bounds
* but much more complicated/more likely to have false positives/negatives 
<hr class="vspace" />
* for each array or pointer track: 

   * minimum number of elements before/after what it points to

* for each integer track:  

   * minimum bound
   * maximum bound

* similar analysis looking at paths?


### checking array bounds (1) 

<!-- \lstset{
    language=C,style=script,
    moredelim={**[is][\btHL<2>]{~2~}{~end~}},
} -->
  

::: {.r-stack}
![](/static/texfig/bounds1.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1}

![](/static/texfig/bounds1.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}


:::


### checking array bounds (2) 

<!-- \lstset{
    language=C,style=script,
    moredelim={**[is][\btHL<2>]{~2~}{~end~}},
} -->
  

::: {.r-stack}
![](/static/texfig/bounds2.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1}

![](/static/texfig/bounds2.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2}


:::
